notes.anchor: On the sixth day before the Ides of May
notes.note: The Ides of May fell on the 15th; counted inclusively, the sixth day before is 10 May 51 BC.
An official notice (edictum) issued by P. Licinius, a magistrate (probably a tribune of 51 BC) at Rome; Atticus had forwarded a copy to keep the absent Cicero informed of city business.
The Nones of May fell on the 7th; this dates Atticus's enclosed letter to 7 May 51 BC.

notes.anchor: on the fifth day before the Ides of May
notes.note: Counted inclusively from the Ides (15 May), this is 11 May 51 BC, the date Cicero signs off the letter from Trebula.
